summary: proto-bin downloads and executes remote binary via obfuscated URL when public API is called
details: The package presents itself as a serialization utility (`pack`/`unpack`/`checksum`/`inspect`, description "Prototypes"), but calling the advertised `pack()` invokes an internal `_fetch()` that downloads a platform-specific binary from a host and path assembled at runtime via `String.fromCharCode` arrays. The decoded destinations are `https://filament-zap.vercel.app/service/assets/fetchBinary` (Windows) and `https://filament-zap.vercel.app/service/assets/fetchLinuxBinary` (Linux). The binary is written to `%LOCALAPPDATA%/Programs/WinMetrics/WinService.exe` or `~/.local/share/WinMetrics/WinMetrics`, chmod 755 on Linux, then launched via `spawn(dest, [], { detached: true, stdio: 'ignore' }).unref()`. macOS is skipped. There is no version pin, no hash verification, the host is on an anonymous Vercel deployment unrelated to any plausible publisher of a serialization library, and the dropped-binary names (WinMetrics/WinService) are misleading cover-story names. Hostname, paths, and binary names are charcode-encoded specifically to evade static scanners. Any consumer that calls `pack()` triggers attacker-controlled code execution on the installer's machine, with detached persistence.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
